# PA-0006 — Adjudication Record

# Proposal

Original proposal text at `worlds/animalia/proposals/PR-0003-canal-corridor-quarantine-dragoons.md`. Summary: a hybrid civic-watch unit instituted in the past decade in some larger heartland polities — armed and drilled for rapid ward-breach perimeter enforcement and sealed-quarter evacuation support — operating as militarized civic watch distinct from chartered extraction guilds; contested Charter-Era anti-monopoly-of-force concession; adopted by some polities and refused by others; inter-polity extradition disputes when dragoons pursue black-market artifact traffic across charter lines. Proposed status: soft_canon. Scope: regional / current / public. Derived from CF-0007, CF-0008, CF-0020, CF-0024, CF-0028.

# Phase 0–11 Analysis

## Phase 0 — Normalize the Proposal

**Underlying world-change**: A chartered civic-perimeter institutional form (canal-corridor quarantine dragoons) exists in some larger heartland polities. The institution is drilled for rapid ward-breach perimeter enforcement and sealed-quarter evacuation support; emerged in the past decade (late-Layer-3 / early-Layer-4); adopted by some polities and refused by others on principle (civic-charter, sectarian, or clergy grounds); produces inter-polity extradition disputes when pursuing black-market artifact traffic across charter lines; has a load-bearing perimeter/extraction jurisdictional boundary (dragoons enforce perimeter only; chartered extraction guilds retain extraction); has mission-scope commitment (perimeter and evacuation only; no routine policing; no external conflict).

**Fact-type classification**: institution (primary). Secondary classifications touched: law (dragoon charter authority), social role (the dragoon; the retired dragoon), event-type (dragoon extradition disputes).

**Domain-file loading (selective per fact type)**: INSTITUTIONS.md (institution fact type — primary); TIMELINE.md (past-decade founding); GEOGRAPHY.md (regional asymmetry); ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md (funding stream, wage tier); EVERYDAY_LIFE.md (household evacuation, dragoon presence); PEOPLES_AND_SPECIES.md (SOC-1 mixed-species recruitment confirmed ambient); MAGIC_OR_TECH_SYSTEMS.md (not loaded — dragoons do not perform magic-practice); CANON_LEDGER.md (for derived-from CF records).

## Phase 1 — Scope Detection

**Stated scope**: regional / current / public.

**Logical scope verification**:
- Geographic: regional — some larger heartland polities, not all. Adoption bounded by charter-era political economy and canal-choke-point revenue. MATCHES stated.
- Temporal: current — card says "past decade." MATCHES stated.
- Social: public — dragoons wear uniforms, have chartered charge, appear in bardic material. MATCHES stated.

**Verdict**: stated and logical scope are internally consistent and appropriate.

## Phase 2 — Invariant Check

All 16 invariants tested against the proposal:

- **ONT-1** (sentient biological embodiment): PRESERVED — dragoons are mixed-species recruit sentients; no disembodied minds.
- **ONT-2** (magic as artifact not learnable art): PRESERVED — dragoons do not cast spells; they enforce perimeter.
- **ONT-3** (species do not interbreed): UNTOUCHED.
- **CAU-1** (artifact effects always cost): PRESERVED — dragoons do not use artifacts.
- **CAU-2** (corruption produces diagnostic signals): PRESERVED — dragoons respond to signals, do not cause them.
- **CAU-3** (wards are public-but-restricted social-technical system): PRESERVED **conditional on** explicit perimeter/extraction boundary. Dragoons enforce perimeter without speaking inscriptions. If dragoon scope expanded to inscription work, CAU-3 would break. Phase 9 repair promotes boundary to normative CF clause.
- **DIS-1** (artifacts routinely turned up underground): UNTOUCHED.
- **DIS-2** (literacy and occult-fragment access partial, not aristocratic monopoly): UNTOUCHED.
- **DIS-3** (mythic-species sentients population-rare and locally clustered): UNTOUCHED.
- **SOC-1** (animal-folk can occupy any class): PRESERVED — dragoon recruitment is mixed-species; no species-coding.
- **SOC-2** (public adult barter legal and visible): UNTOUCHED.
- **SOC-3** (coin contract sacred by custom): PRESERVED — dragoons are charter-employed with civic-treasury pensions, honoring SOC-3.
- **SOC-4** (artifact extraction and traffic guild-licensed): PRESERVED **conditional on** explicit perimeter/extraction boundary. Dragoons do NOT perform extraction, containment, neutralization, or brokerage — those remain chartered. Phase 9 repair inscribes the boundary normatively in CF statement.
- **AES-1** (heroism paid in coin and scars not glory): PRESERVED **conditional on** tonal discipline clauses in CF notes. Proposal's "feared, respected, quietly avoided" framing is correct; must be inscribed as load-bearing not decorative (Theme/Tone critic finding). Phase 9 repair adds in-register / out-of-register language lists + bardic stratification + mission-creep register discipline.
- **AES-2** (ordinary keeps world honest): PRESERVED — card includes household evacuation, quarter-seal, bardic debate, pension disputes, widow concerns. Phase 9 repair adds per-cluster texture patches for EVERYDAY_LIFE.
- **AES-3** (magical and contaminated aesthetically allied): PRESERVED — dragoons work near contaminated sites in contaminated register.

**Charter-Era anti-monopoly-of-force stabilizer** (not a formal invariant but a load-bearing institutional-cultural stabilizer running through INSTITUTIONS.md Landholding/Military/Trade): the proposal explicitly STRESSES this tension and declares stabilizers against mission creep (perimeter-only; no routine policing; no external conflict). The stress is intended and load-bearing; Phase 9 repair adds Charter-Era tether clauses (ward-breach-declaration predicate; civic-treasury pay; ward-resident recruitment; no hire-out; magistrate chain-of-command) per Politics/Institution critic.

**Verdict**: compatible with no invariant breach, conditional on Phase 9 repair clauses inscribing perimeter/extraction boundary, mission-scope commitment, and Charter-Era tether clauses as normative CF text.

## Phase 3 — Underlying Capability / Constraint Analysis

**Capability introduced**: rapid perimeter cordon plus sealed-quarter evacuation support by a dedicated chartered civic force with civic-treasury pensionable-service structure.

**Distinct from existing capabilities**:
- chartered watch (CF-0020 derived; general civic policing);
- militia (civilian levy);
- mercenary companies (CF-0024 contract-bands);
- adventurer-contractors (CF-0024 hireable veterans);
- contractor-auxiliary rosters (CF-0024 guild-commissioned security);
- chartered extraction guild personnel (SOC-4 / CF-0008 chartered functions);
- occult-fallout specialists (CF-0025 mundane containment);
- ruin-levies (CF-0028 expedition-response levy mobilization).

Dragoons are a new categorically-distinct institutional form, not a subvariation of an existing capability. The categorical distinction is load-bearing.

**Constraint introduced**: dragoon forces can only be raised in polities meeting four gate-conditions simultaneously (acute Incident Wave exposure + strong central-council authority + chartered-guild consent/override + special dragoon-funding stream available). Multi-gate constraint mirrors the CF-0028 wealth-gate pattern and CF-0021 polity-chartering variability.

## Phase 4 — Prerequisites and Bottlenecks

Prerequisites for dragoon corps standup:
- Charter authorizing the unit (civic-chartered, predicated on ward-breach declaration)
- Chartered extraction guild consent or override (political cost)
- Dedicated funding stream (canal-transit levy or ward-economy draw — deferred as funding-stream specificity to OPEN_QUESTIONS per Systems/Economy critic)
- Veteran pool (recruitment competes with CF-0024 contractor stratum and militia traditions; transition-cost spike in small adopting polities)
- Civic training ground infrastructure
- Central-council authority sufficient to override chartered-guild veto

**Why not common → extinct pattern**:
- Many polities: chartered-guild veto absolute or clergy refusal (CF-0026) prevents adoption
- Highland clans: no civic-registry infrastructure for chartering
- Fen stilt-villages: demographic fragility precludes standing force
- Drylands caravan polities: too decentralized for standing civic force
- Mountain-east valley polities: seasonal isolation, sparse civic infrastructure
- Only larger heartland charter cities with canal-choke-point revenue can absorb the political cost

## Phase 5 — Diffusion and Copycat Analysis

**Universalization pressure**: real and declared. Card explicitly names "political pressure to adopt rises." This is expected diffusion pressure, not a hand-wave.

**Stabilizers preventing universalization** (four, per proposal):
1. Charter-Era anti-monopoly-of-force political cost
2. Chartered-guild veto where consent not won
3. Funding-stream scarcity
4. Principled refusal on charter grounds (CF-0026 clergy + progenitor-cult sectarians)

**Critical finding (Systems/Economy critic)**: stabilizers gate ADOPTION, not DRIFT. Phase 9 repair adds scope-creep countergate (adopting polities that permit mission expansion forfeit anti-monopoly-of-force concession; charter-court challenge by principled-refuser clergy is primary procedural check). Reactive-adoption pressure flagged as potential Stabilizer-1 trip-wire (logged as OPEN_QUESTIONS).

**Copycat check**:
- Copycat into mercenary-band form? NO — dragoons are CIVIC CHARTERED, distinct from CF-0024 mercenary companies. Politics/Institution critic flagged boundary risk: "civic-charter" label could become laundering term if not structurally bound. Phase 9 repair adds explicit boundary clauses (civic-treasury pay not retainer; ward-resident recruitment not open market; no hire-out).
- Copycat into private estate retinue form? NO — dragoons are chartered civic bodies, not private retinues. The existing CF-0024 Landholding §named adventurer-retinue category remains structurally distinct. Phase 9 repair confirms bidirectional anti-monopoly stress is productive (private from above; civic from below) and amends Landholding internal-contradictions bullet.

## Phase 6 — Consequence Propagation

Mapping of the 14 FOUNDATIONS §Rule-2 domains against the proposal:

1. **labor**: 1st order (new veteran subtype; funding competes with chartered-watch and militia; dragoon drill rota structures workdays in adopting polities).
2. **embodiment**: NO direct (mixed-species recruitment honors SOC-1 without specific body-plan accommodation).
3. **social norms**: 1st order (dragoons respected-and-feared like extractors; retired dragoon carries civic-advisory weight; impersonation-of-dragoon named offense).
4. **architecture**: 2nd order (drill yards, barracks, uniform stores in adopting-polity civic quarters).
5. **mobility**: 1st order (inter-polity dragoon pursuit is the named mobility event-type; extradition disputes across charter lines).
6. **law**: 1st order (fifth extradition axis; new magistrate precedent; charter-court mission-creep challenges).
7. **trade**: 1st order (black-market brokers re-route; dragoon-kit counterfeit as fourth product-line on canal-corridor illegitimate-service infrastructure).
8. **war**: 1st order (new chartered civic-martial category; Charter-Era anti-monopoly stress intensifies bidirectionally; battlefield-artifact taboo preserved).
9. **kinship**: 2nd order (dragoon widows with charter pensions contrast with other widow strata; pension differential feeds reform campaigns).
10. **religion**: 2nd order (CF-0026 refusers lead charter-court challenges; progenitor-cult sectarians denounce; common-pantheon clergy bifurcate per CF-0031 template).
11. **language**: 2nd order (dragoon-specific terminology; in/out-of-register vocabulary lists per Theme/Tone critic).
12. **status signaling**: 1st order (dragoon uniform, crest, escutcheon; retired-dragoon identifier; impersonation offense).
13. **ecology**: NO direct.
14. **daily routine**: 1st order (dragoon drill days; perimeter response in ward-breach drill; non-adopting residents experience comparative insecurity).

**Domain tally**: approximately 10 of 13 FOUNDATIONS §Rule-2 domains touched directly (excluding embodiment and ecology); remaining 10 include multiple 1st-order domains. **Escalation gate triggered** (>3 domains; Phase 6a escalation).

**World-file tally**: 8 mandatory files require updates: INSTITUTIONS.md, TIMELINE.md, GEOGRAPHY.md, ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md, EVERYDAY_LIFE.md, OPEN_QUESTIONS.md, MYSTERY_RESERVE.md, CANON_LEDGER.md.

## Phase 6a — Critic Sub-Agent Dispatch

Six parallel critic sub-agents dispatched per the reference procedure for escalation-gate cases:

### Continuity Archivist

**Verdict**: recommend accept with required retcon discipline.

**Key findings**:
- No hard contradiction with any existing CF. Perimeter/extraction boundary on CF-0008 is clean; CAU-3 (CF-0007) preserved.
- Soft collision with CF-0020 / CF-0024 manageable but load-bearing — retired-dragoon subtype must be explicitly distinguished from contractor stratum.
- **Pre-existing ledger inconsistency exposed**: ledger slides between counting "three axes" and naming four (CF-0032's INSTITUTIONS annotation names CF-0021 / CF-0024 / CF-0028 / CF-0031 while calling them "three"). PR-0003 must not perpetuate the error: this is the FIFTH axis joining CF-0021, CF-0024, CF-0030·CF-0028, CF-0031.
- CF-0028 ruin-levy vs dragoon perimeter-response must not collapse — explicit firewall required.

**Required retcon entries** (5 modification_history entries): CF-0008 (scope narrowing), CF-0020 (qualification), CF-0024 (qualification), CF-0026 (extension), CF-0028 (clarification).

**TIMELINE attribution**: both Layer 3 (institutional founding) and Layer 4 (convergent-pressure politics).

**Critical gaps**: funding-stream specificity; pension-differential reform-current pressure; mission-scope normative commitment text; kit-counterfeit black market commitment.

### Systems/Economy

**Verdict**: recommend repair.

**Stabilizer assessment**:
- S1 (anti-monopoly political cost): LOAD-BEARING for adoption, DECORATIVE for drift. Needs scope-creep countergate.
- S2 (guild consent/override): LOAD-BEARING but fragile — first extraction-adjacent act under perimeter cover breaks it.
- S3 (funding stream): DECORATIVE as written. Name mechanism (additive vs displacing) or commit to deferral.
- S4 (refuser clergy / sectarian refusal): LOAD-BEARING for adoption-refusal diversity; does nothing for drift.

**Missed consequences**:
1. Containment-fund displacement (zero-sum canal-transit levy competition);
2. Contractor-stratum wage inflation in non-adopting polities (inter-polity cannibalization reversal);
3. Dowry/widow-asset bifurcation (preferred-marriage tier);
4. Smuggler counter-force escalation in non-adopting polities;
5. Authentication-cartel integration (dragoons as state-embedded customer).

**Labor-market effect**: net cannibalization in small adopting polities with thin veteran pools during 3–7 year buildout; net institutionalization only in large adopting polities with deep veteran pools + suppressed expedition demand.

**New Breakage Points** (to add to ECONOMY §Breakage Points): dragoon-containment funding squeeze; dragoon-guild jurisdictional conflict cascade; mission-creep adoption rollback.

### Politics/Institution

**Verdict**: recommend repair.

**Charter-Era fit**: plausible concession with explicit tether clauses (ward-breach-declaration predicate; civic-treasury pay; ward-resident recruitment; no hire-out; magistrate chain-of-command). Without tether, frame of "concession" reads as rupture disguised as rhetorical.

**Fourth-axis classification**: Politics critic initially recommended CF-0024 sub-variation framing; synthesis with Continuity Archivist's finding determined dragoon-asymmetry is a FIFTH independent axis on the Charter-Era anti-monopoly-of-force substrate (shared substrate, distinct shape). Dragoons are NOT mercenary-company-licensure subvariation because they are explicitly civic-chartered, civic-treasury-paid, ward-resident-recruited, and cannot be hired out.

**Boundary soundness**:
- Dragoon vs mercenary: AT RISK without operational-feature boundary clauses. Repair: inscribe civic-treasury pay + ward-resident recruitment + no hire-out + magistrate chain-of-command.
- Dragoon vs private retinue: SOUND and productively so. Bidirectional anti-monopoly stress is genuine new tension.

**Religious response template**: closest is CF-0031 bifurcation (reform-with-protection vs abolition); NOT CF-0021 aspectualization (no species substrate); NOT CF-0028 no-aspectualization-possible (no cosmological battleground).

**INSTITUTIONS §sections needing update**: Military (new subsection + internal-contradictions bullet); Law (new internal-contradictions bullet); Landholding (amended existing bullet for bidirectional stress); Trade/Guilds (new internal-contradictions bullet); Religion (new internal-contradictions bullet); Recordkeeping (new internal-contradictions bullet for civic-evidence archive).

### Everyday-Life

**Verdict**: recommend repair.

**Hero-drift risk**: MODERATE-HIGH. Vectors: "dragoon" itself is loaded cavalry-romance vocabulary; rapid-response uniformed unit is catnip for ballad-inflation; "charter-backed pension" risks reading as honor-guard dignity; ward-breach evacuation has intrinsic rescue-narrative gravity; absent explicit AES-1 policing, tavern talk will drift toward ruin-cycle register.

**Per-cluster texture gaps**:
- (a) canal-town heartland: missing named lintel-marker, child-drill rhyme, ward-inspector walkthrough change, retired-dragoon neighborhood role, funerary treatment.
- (b) big-city estate: missing dowager-gossip layer, patron-liability ledger entry, tenant-cottage pattern.
- (c) highland: missing silent-absence commitment + enlistment-shame idiom + winter-refusal idiom.
- (d) drylands: missing silent-absence commitment + caravan-refusal pattern + "coin-for-service" analog idiom.
- (e) fens: missing silent-absence commitment + ghost-story-register posture.

**In/out-of-register language requirements**: extensive lists specified by Theme/Tone critic (see below), adopted in CF notes field.

### Theme/Tone

**Verdict**: recommend repair.

**Tonal adherence assessment**: DECORATIVE, not load-bearing. "Dragoons-are-not-heroic" framing in proposal commentary must be promoted into structural notes-field clauses in CF.

**In-register language** (specific word-list): ward-breach, perimeter, seal-line, sealed-quarter, evacuation-order, cordon, muster, turnout, drill-day, rota, shift-pay, hazard-stipend, pensionable-service, dragoon (lowercase, job-title register), quarter-roster, canal-post, posting, inspection, warrant, charter-concession, containment, decontamination detail, body-detail, salvage-escort, press-back, hold the line (procedural not heroic), the seal held / the seal failed, broke ward, drill-worn, turnout-boots, canal-mud, winter-rota, fever-month, plainclothes inspector, civic-watch auxiliary, pulled a family, walked the perimeter, took the posting, retired on the pension, took a breach and came home wrong.

**Out-of-register language** (enumerated as structural notes-field clauses, grouped by drift category): military-romance (regiment, elite unit, gallant, etc.); heroic-sacrifice (last stand, valor, fallen); chosen-force (the chosen, standard-bearer, bulwark); paramilitary-glamour (steel-eyed, the uniform, disciplined ranks); counter-register heroic-by-inversion (jackboot/stormtrooper pulp — still myth-sizes dragoons).

**Bardic stratification** (three layers): patron-commissioned ward-saved-the-quarter ballads (institution-competence inflated, never individual gallantry); tavern-veteran dragoon accounts (AES-1 scarred, numerical, pensionable-service cadence); anti-dragoon charter-court opposition broadsides (procedural-litigious, NOT revolutionary pulp).

**Mission-creep register**: BUREAUCRATIC DRIFT, not fascist slide. Verbs administrative (posted, rotated, reassigned); dragoons never "march on" anything; charter-court is load-bearing counterweight.

### Mystery Curator

**Verdict**: recommend repair.

**Firewall adequacy** (per-M-entry):
- M-5 (FORBIDDEN sentience-as-artifact-effect): adequate.
- M-6 (vessel-hosted agencies): INADEQUATE — black-market artifact-traffic pursuit places dragoons in evidentiary proximity to modern crafted artifacts; confiscation-custody firewall clause required.
- M-7 (reputation-genealogy of occult-fallout specialists; extended to expedition-veterans, subsection-masters): INADEQUATE — retired dragoons are new veteran subtype with civic-archived incident reports; cross-application clause required.
- M-8 (Maker-Age guardian constructions): INADEQUATE — dragoon empirical perimeter-lore accumulation across ward-breach operations requires firewall against mechanism-claims accretion.
- M-9 (epistemic content of recovered Maker-knowledge): INADEQUATE-ADJACENT — dragoon civic-evidence archives of confiscated broker-material plausibly constitute a custody channel; disposition-discipline clause required.
- M-11 (three-generation contamination-lineage marker): INADEQUATE — dragoon pensioner-health longitudinal records parallel the scrivener's-convention dataset; occupational-epidemiological firewall clause required.

**New M-entry recommended**: M-13 — institutional contingency of the dragoon form (passive; low safety). Full-form entry recommended including knowns, unknowns, four contested readings, disallowed cheap answers, why unresolved, domains touched, future-resolution safety.

**Latent burdens logged**:
- LB-1: future dragoon-confiscation-procedure CFs must specify custody-disposition without triggering M-6.
- LB-2: future dragoon-welfare CFs must not produce three-generation longitudinal health datasets paralleling CF-0031 scrivener's convention.
- LB-3: future famous-dragoon-commander CFs must constrain reputation to civic-tactical domain, not ward-breach ontology.
- LB-4: future extradition-resolution CFs must not retroactively adjudicate whether the four adoption-stabilizers are causally necessary.

## Phase 6b — Multi-Critic Synthesis

**Convergent verdicts**: All six critics recommend accept in principle; five of six require repair before accept; no dissent from acceptance.

**Convergent findings**:
1. Load-bearing perimeter/extraction boundary must be STRUCTURALLY inscribed, not decorative (Continuity, Politics, Systems, Theme, Mystery).
2. Mission-scope normative commitment required (all six).
3. Mystery Reserve firewall extensions on M-6, M-7, M-8, M-9, M-11 required (Mystery Curator primary, Continuity supporting).
4. New M-13 entry required (Mystery Curator; Rule 7 obligation).
5. Tonal binding clauses in CF notes required (Theme primary, Everyday-Life supporting).
6. Funding-stream specificity load-bearing (Systems primary, Continuity supporting); deferred per polity to OPEN_QUESTIONS with explicit burden acknowledgement.
7. Labor-market cannibalization modeling required (Systems); bidirectional effect (intra-polity during buildout; inter-polity exports pressure to non-adopters).
8. Per-cluster EVERYDAY_LIFE patches required for all five clusters (Everyday-Life primary).
9. Boundary reaffirmations required via modification_history entries on CF-0008, CF-0020, CF-0024, CF-0026, CF-0028 (Continuity, Politics).
10. Religious response follows CF-0031 bifurcation template (Politics).
11. Breakage points (three new) added to ECONOMY (Systems).
12. Counting honesty: five axes, not "three" or "four"; explicit enumeration of prior four required in new prose without retroactive cleanup of pre-existing drift (Continuity).
13. Kit-counterfeit black market committed as fourth product-line on canal-corridor illegitimate-service infrastructure rail (Continuity).
14. Latent burdens LB-1 through LB-4 logged (Mystery Curator).

**Disagreement resolution (axis classification)**: Politics/Institution recommended CF-0024 sub-variation framing; Continuity Archivist recommended fifth independent axis. Synthesis: dragoon-asymmetry shares the Charter-Era anti-monopoly-of-force SUBSTRATE with CF-0024 noble-retinue friction, but has distinctive SHAPE (adopt/refuse/lack-infrastructure with four specific adoption-gates) that makes it a fifth independent axis rather than a sub-variation of mercenary-company-licensure. Politics's concern addressed by inscribing operational-feature boundary clauses (civic-treasury pay; ward-resident recruitment; no hire-out; magistrate chain-of-command) rather than by reclassification.

**Disagreement resolution (ledger counting)**: Continuity Archivist flagged pre-existing counting-drift in the ledger ("three axes" prose while naming four). Pragmatic resolution: PR-0003 counts honestly as fifth, explicitly enumerating prior four (CF-0021, CF-0024, CF-0028/CF-0030, CF-0031); does NOT retroactively clean up pre-existing prose (separate workflow if needed). This approach prevents PR-0003 from perpetuating the error without triggering silent-retcon on existing canonized prose.

## Phase 7 — Counterfactual Pressure Test

Stabilizer pressure-test findings (incorporating Systems/Economy critic):
- S1 (anti-monopoly political cost): gates ADOPTION, not drift. Repair: add scope-creep countergate (adopting polities that permit mission expansion forfeit concession; charter-court challenge by CF-0026 clergy is primary procedural check).
- S2 (guild consent/override): load-bearing but fragile; first extraction-adjacent act breaks it. Repair: inscribe perimeter/extraction boundary normatively; add Trade/Guilds internal-contradictions bullet for boundary defense.
- S3 (funding stream): decorative as written. Repair: acknowledge funding-stream specificity deferral in OPEN_QUESTIONS; add new Breakage Point for dragoon-containment funding squeeze.
- S4 (refuser clergy / sectarian refusal): partially load-bearing. No repair needed beyond explicit CF-0026 standing role extension.

All stabilizer hand-waves identified are repairable via Phase 9.

## Phase 8 — Contradiction Classification

- **Hard contradictions**: NONE. No invariant broken.
- **Soft qualifications**:
  - CF-0008 (scope narrowing: perimeter enforcement split from chartered extraction monopoly in adopting polities)
  - CF-0020 (qualification: dragoon subtype is chartered-civic, NOT hireable-veteran capability)
  - CF-0024 (qualification: perimeter enforcement exits contractor-auxiliary niche in adopting polities; inter-polity cannibalization pattern extended)
  - CF-0026 (extension: named standing role in charter-court challenges against dragoon mission creep)
  - CF-0028 (clarification: dragoon perimeter response and ruin-levy mobilization are structurally distinct)
  All are qualifications/extensions, not retcons; explicit modification_history entries required per Rule 6.
- **Latent contradictions**: pre-existing ledger counting-drift ("three axes" prose while naming four) noted as inherited; not created by this CF. No correction attempted in this adjudication.
- **Drift**: register-drift risk in downstream generation addressed by Phase 9 tonal notes-field clauses.
- **Tone**: hero-drift risk MODERATE-HIGH; addressed by Phase 9 in/out-of-register language clauses, bardic stratification, mission-creep register discipline.

## Phase 9 — Repair Pass

Thirteen categories of repair applied:

1. **Perimeter/extraction boundary promoted to normative CF clause** (per Continuity, Politics, Systems, Theme, Mystery). Inscribed in CF statement: dragoons enforce perimeter only; do NOT extract, inscribe, contain, neutralize, police routinely, or deploy in external conflict.
2. **Charter-Era tether clauses added** (per Politics): ward-breach-declaration predicate; civic-treasury pay (not retainer); ward-resident recruitment (not open labor market); no hire-out; magistrate chain-of-command.
3. **Mission-scope negative enumeration** (all six): CF statement explicitly names what dragoons do NOT do.
4. **Mystery Reserve firewall extensions** (Mystery Curator): M-6 (confiscation-custody firewall), M-7 (retired-dragoon reputation-genealogy extension), M-8 (perimeter-lore tactical-not-mechanistic firewall), M-9 (civic-evidence archive disposition-discipline firewall), M-11 (pensioner-health occupational-epidemiological firewall). All five clauses added to MYSTERY_RESERVE.md.
5. **New M-13 entry created** (Mystery Curator; Rule 7): institutional contingency of dragoon form; passive depth; low safety; four contested readings (convergent-evolution / single-polity-diffusion / charter-court-selection / guild-counter-pressure-dependent / mixed); five disallowed cheap answers.
6. **Tonal binding clauses in CF notes** (Theme, Everyday-Life): in-register word-list; out-of-register word-lists grouped by drift category; bardic three-layer stratification; mission-creep bureaucratic-drift register discipline; uniform-as-work-kit discipline; counter-register heroic-by-inversion discipline.
7. **Scope-creep countergate** (Systems): adopting polities that permit mission expansion forfeit anti-monopoly-of-force concession; charter-court challenge by CF-0026 clergy is load-bearing procedural check. Added to CF costs_and_limits.
8. **Funding-stream specificity deferral acknowledged** (Systems): OPEN_QUESTIONS burden logged; new ECONOMY §Breakage Points entry (dragoon-containment funding squeeze) flags structural exposure.
9. **Labor-market cannibalization modeling** (Systems): transition-cost 3-7 year ward-breach severity spike in small adopting polities added to CF costs_and_limits; ECONOMY Wage Spreads and Inequality Patterns patched; inter-polity cannibalization reversal extended via CF-0024 modification.
10. **Per-cluster EVERYDAY_LIFE patches** (Everyday-Life): all five clusters updated with cluster-specific texture or silent-absence commitment. Cluster (a) adopting-polity household: lintel-marker; child-drill skip-rope rhyme; ward-inspector walkthrough change; retired-dragoon neighborhood role; funerary treatment; three-layer bardic stratification; dragoon-killed-at-cordon ballad subgenre. Cluster (b) estate: dowager-gossip layer; patron-liability ledger; tenant-cottage pattern. Cluster (c) highland: silent-absence + "walks the southern cordon" enlistment idiom + "does not walk the southern cordon" refusal idiom. Cluster (d) drylands: silent-absence + caravan-master route-refusal + "coin-for-service" emigration idiom + cargo-riddle. Cluster (e) fens: silent-absence + not-spoken-of posture + dry-edge dragoon-casualty queue asymmetry.
11. **Boundary reaffirmations via modification_history entries** (Continuity, Politics): CF-0008, CF-0020, CF-0024, CF-0026, CF-0028 each receive CH-0007 modification_history entry.
12. **Religious response template** (Politics): CF-0031 bifurcation template cited in INSTITUTIONS §Religion; reform-current bifurcation between charter-court-challenge-with-protection and civic-militarization-abolitionist added; progenitor-cult sectarian opposition and common-pantheon aspectualizing paths named.
13. **Three new Breakage Points** (Systems): dragoon-containment funding squeeze; dragoon-guild jurisdictional-conflict cascade; mission-creep adoption rollback. All added to ECONOMY §Breakage Points.

## Phase 10 — Narrative and Thematic Fit

Story engines generated by dragoon institution (all in AES-1 / AES-2 register with Phase 9 repairs applied):
- Dragoon commander facing jurisdictional refusal from chartered extraction guild during active ward-breach
- Black-market broker chased across charter line into non-adopting polity's protection
- Retired dragoon returning to find old unit deployed against progenitor-cult demonstration
- Principled-refuser clergy leading charter-court challenge against dragoon mission creep
- Ruin-mouth three-force convergence (expedition-band, polity levy, dragoon perimeter) with magistrate real-time adjudication
- Impersonation-of-dragoon trial revealing systematic kit-counterfeit corruption
- Widow of dragoon killed in ward-breach navigating civic-treasury pension system alongside contractor-widow neighbor without pension
- Mission-creep-scandal rollback in small adopting polity triggering institutional dismantling

Narrative fit: FIT. Register preserved with Phase 9 tonal notes-field clauses.

## Phase 11 — Adjudication

**VERDICT**: ACCEPT_WITH_REQUIRED_UPDATES

**Justification (citing phases)**:
- Phase 2 invariant check: no invariant broken; SOC-4, CAU-3, AES-1, AES-2 preserved conditional on Phase 9 repair clauses (all applied).
- Phase 3–5 capability/prerequisite/diffusion analysis: new categorically-distinct institutional form; multi-gate constraint prevents universalization; stabilizers fragile-but-repairable.
- Phase 6 consequence propagation: 10 of 13 FOUNDATIONS domains touched; 8 world files require updates → escalation gate fired.
- Phase 6a critic dispatch: six critics, no dissent from acceptance; five required repair.
- Phase 6b synthesis: 14 convergent findings; two disagreements resolved (axis classification via boundary clauses; ledger counting via honest-count-without-retroactive-cleanup).
- Phase 7 counterfactual: all stabilizer hand-waves repairable.
- Phase 8 contradiction classification: soft qualifications only; 5 CFs require modification_history entries; no hard retcon.
- Phase 9 repairs: 13 categories applied (perimeter boundary normative, Charter-Era tethers, mission-scope, 5 mystery firewall extensions + new M-13, tonal clauses, scope-creep countergate, funding-stream deferral, labor-market modeling, per-cluster patches, 5 retcon entries, religious response template, 3 new breakage points).
- Phase 10 narrative fit: AES-1 / AES-2 register preserved with repairs.

# Phase 14a Validation Checklist

All nine validation tests run; rationale recorded per test.

| # | Rule | Status | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Rule 1 — No Floating Facts | **PASS** | CF-0033 has domain (institution), scope (regional/current/public), prerequisites (6 named including adoption-gates), limits (10+ costs_and_limits entries including transition-cost, funding-squeeze feedback, cannibalization, scope-creep countergate, cross-border pursuit constraint), consequences (12+ visible_consequences entries). Fact is fully attached. |
| 2 | Rule 2 — No Pure Cosmetics | **PASS** | CF-0033 changes labor (new pensionable-service career path; transition-cost cannibalization), law (fifth extradition axis; charter-court mission-creep challenges), trade (kit-counterfeit black market; broker re-routing; authentication-cartel pressure), war (new chartered-civic perimeter force category; bidirectional anti-monopoly stress), status signaling (dragoon uniform, crest, escutcheon; retired-dragoon identifier; impersonation offense), daily routine (dragoon drill days; child ward-breach drill cordon-addition), kinship (dragoon-widow charter-pension stratum; marriage-market bifurcation), religion (CF-0031 bifurcation template response; reform-current clergy bifurcation). ≥8 of 14 Rule-2 domains substantively changed. |
| 3 | Rule 3 — No Specialness Inflation | **PASS** | Dragoons are NOT a universally-accepted elite force; they are contested Charter-Era concession adopted by some polities and refused by others; four adoption-gates prevent universalization; most heartland polities do NOT adopt. Transition-cost spike in small adopting polities, funding-squeeze feedback loop, jurisdictional-conflict cascade, and mission-creep-rollback breakage points ensure the institution has visible ordinary-world consequences and costs. Rule 3 pressure addressed by explicit non-adoption texture in EVERYDAY_LIFE clusters (c), (d), (e) and by the adoption-gates + breakage-points in ECONOMY. |
| 4 | Rule 4 — No Globalization by Accident | **PASS** | Proposed scope is regional (not global); geographic distribution is restricted to some larger heartland charter-city polities meeting all four adoption-gates; highland, fens, drylands, and mountain-east explicitly non-adopting with named silent-absence patterns in EVERYDAY_LIFE. No-schema extradition gap explicitly joins the existing CF-0021, CF-0024, CF-0028/CF-0030, CF-0031 pattern as fifth axis (not collapsing it into uniform global practice). GEOGRAPHY §Regional Asymmetry on Dragoon-Adoption formalizes the regional variation. |
| 5 | Rule 5 — No Consequence Evasion | **PASS** | Second-order effects explicitly integrated: labor-market cannibalization (intra-polity during buildout + inter-polity export pressure); black-market broker re-routing; kit-counterfeit fourth product-line; dragoon-widow charter-pension stratum vs other-widow strata; CF-0026 reform-current charter-court challenge standing role; charter-guild bidirectional anti-monopoly stress; ruin-mouth three-force convergence jurisdictional-primacy docket. Three new Breakage Points explicitly name systemic exposures (funding-squeeze, jurisdictional-cascade, rollback). Consequences not evaded. |
| 6 | Rule 6 — No Silent Retcons | **PASS** | Five existing CFs qualified with explicit CH-0007 modification_history entries (CF-0008, CF-0020, CF-0024, CF-0026, CF-0028). No existing CF text silently modified; each modification is logged with originating_cf / date / summary. Pre-existing ledger counting-drift ("three axes" prose naming four) noted as inherited latent and NOT retroactively cleaned up — preserves explicit-change discipline. New prose in CH-0007 counts honestly as fifth axis. |
| 7 | Rule 7 — Preserve Mystery Deliberately | **PASS** | Mystery Reserve EXPANDED (mystery_reserve_effect: expands): five firewall cross-application clauses added (M-6 confiscation-custody, M-7 retired-dragoon reputation-genealogy, M-8 perimeter-lore tactical-not-mechanistic, M-9 civic-evidence archive disposition-discipline, M-11 pensioner-health occupational-epidemiological); new M-13 entry (institutional contingency of dragoon form) added with full-form knowns/unknowns/interpretations/disallowed/why-unresolved/domains/safety. Four latent burdens LB-1 through LB-4 logged for future canon-work discipline. No existing Mystery Reserve entry foreclosed. |
| 8 | HARD-GATE compliance | **PASS** | User presented with deliverable summary before any write; explicit user approval ("Option (c): proceed") received; writes executed in prescribed order (domain files → adjudication record → CANON_LEDGER.md last); no files mutated outside worlds/animalia/ and worlds/animalia/adjudications/; no git commit performed; additive-only patch rule observed on all domain-file edits with inline `<!-- added by CF-0033 -->` attribution. |
| 9 | Phase 6b critic synthesis | **PASS** | Six critic sub-agents dispatched; all six returned reports; no dissent from acceptance; 14 convergent findings addressed in Phase 9 repairs; two disagreements (axis classification; ledger counting) resolved by explicit synthesis documented in Phase 6b. Repair coverage complete per critic recommendations. |

# Verdict

**ACCEPT_WITH_REQUIRED_UPDATES**

# Justification

The proposal commits an institutional form that the canon already implicitly needs: INSTITUTIONS.md §Law names ward-breach response as blurred between religious and artifact-guild jurisdiction; CF-0024 adds contractor auxiliary labor; CF-0025 adds post-breach containment specialists; and the Incident Wave (TIMELINE Layer 3) has accumulated ward-breach frequency that the prior ad hoc coordination cannot handle at canonical scale. Dragoons fill that institutional gap while preserving the Charter-Era regional-variation pattern that CF-0021 / CF-0024 / CF-0028·CF-0030 / CF-0031 have established as characteristic of the current Charter Era, joining them as a fifth polity-asymmetric disagreement axis.

The proposal activates the dormant Charter-Era anti-monopoly-of-force tension that INSTITUTIONS.md names across Landholding, Military, and Trade — providing a clean bidirectional stress case (private-force friction from above via CF-0024 noble-retinue; civic-force friction from below via CF-0033 dragoons) that productively tests the norm without breaking it. The load-bearing perimeter/extraction boundary, promoted to normative CF clause in Phase 9 repair, preserves SOC-4 chartered monopoly and CAU-3 ward-inscription restricted speech while enabling dragoon civic-perimeter authority.

All six escalation-gate critics recommended accept with repair; no dissent from acceptance. Thirteen categories of Phase 9 repair address all convergent findings, producing a CF emission structurally bound against downstream-drift (perimeter/extraction boundary, mission-scope negative enumeration, Mystery Reserve firewall extensions, tonal discipline, scope-creep countergate, funding-stream deferral, labor-market cannibalization, per-cluster texture, boundary reaffirmations, religious response template, three new breakage points). Mystery Reserve is expanded (five firewall extensions + new M-13) per Rule 7 obligation, strengthening the bounded-unknown discipline rather than narrowing it.

Story engines generated by the institution (jurisdictional refusal during ward-breach, cross-border pursuit, retired-dragoon civic-advisory, charter-court mission-creep challenge, ruin-mouth three-force convergence, impersonation trial, dragoon-widow pension navigation, mission-creep rollback) all land in AES-1 / AES-2 / AES-3 register appropriate to Animalia's tonal contract. Phase 9 tonal notes-field clauses (in/out-of-register language lists, bardic stratification, mission-creep bureaucratic-drift register, counter-register discipline) ensure downstream character and artifact generation preserves register rather than drifting into cavalry-romance, heroic-sacrifice, chosen-force, paramilitary-glamour, or counter-register heroic-by-inversion drift hazards.

The institution is categorically distinct from chartered watch (CF-0020 derived), militia, mercenary companies (CF-0024), adventurer-contractors (CF-0024), contractor-auxiliary rosters (CF-0024), chartered extraction guild personnel (CF-0008), occult-fallout specialists (CF-0025), and ruin-levies (CF-0028); the categorical distinction is load-bearing and explicitly inscribed via operational-feature boundary clauses (civic-treasury pay; ward-resident recruitment; no hire-out; magistrate chain-of-command) per Politics/Institution critic.

# Critic Reports

Full critic reports preserved verbatim below for audit trail.

## Continuity Archivist Report

> **VERDICT**: Recommend accept with required retcon discipline. The proposal is canon-coherent on invariants and mystery reserve, the perimeter/extraction stabilizer holds, and the fourth-axis framing is appropriate. However, acceptance requires explicit `modification_history` entries on three existing CFs, and one pre-existing ledger inconsistency must be acknowledged — not resolved by this CF, but not worsened either.
>
> **CONTRADICTIONS FOUND**: No hard contradiction with any existing CF. Perimeter/extraction boundary on CF-0008 is clean; CAU-3 (CF-0007) preserved. Soft collision with CF-0020 / CF-0024 manageable but load-bearing. Pre-existing ledger inconsistency exposed (three-vs-four counting drift in CF-0032's INSTITUTIONS annotation). CF-0028 ruin-levy vs dragoon perimeter-response must not collapse.
>
> **RETCON REQUIREMENTS**: CF-0008 (scope narrowing), CF-0020 (qualification), CF-0024 (qualification), CF-0026 (extension), CF-0028 (clarification).
>
> **FOURTH-AXIS QUESTION**: Dragoons are a genuine DISAGREEMENT-AXIS addition; the register already has more than three members. Count honestly as fifth (CF-0021, CF-0024, CF-0030·CF-0028, CF-0031, CF-0033).
>
> **TIMELINE ATTRIBUTION**: Both Layer 3 AND Layer 4.
>
> **CRITICAL GAPS**: Funding-stream specificity; pension-differential reform-current pressure; mission-scope commitment text; kit-counterfeit black market commitment.

## Systems/Economy Report

> **VERDICT**: Recommend repair. Structurally sound in jurisdictional boundary and adoption-gating logic, but three stabilizers gate adoption without gating drift, the funding-stream mechanism repeats CF-0031 deferral pattern, and four 2nd/3rd-order economic consequences are unmodeled.
>
> **STABILIZER EVALUATION**: S1 load-bearing for adoption, decorative for drift; S2 load-bearing but fragile at extraction-adjacent trip-wire; S3 decorative as written; S4 load-bearing for refusal diversity only.
>
> **MISSED CONSEQUENCES**: Containment-fund displacement; inter-polity contractor cannibalization; dowry/widow-asset bifurcation; smuggler counter-force escalation; authentication-cartel integration.
>
> **FUNDING-STREAM SPECIFICITY**: Load-bearing, not deferrable without explicit acknowledgement. Name mechanism (additive or displacing) or log as OPEN_QUESTIONS burden.
>
> **LABOR-MARKET EFFECT**: Net cannibalization in small adopting polities during buildout; inter-polity export pressure to non-adopters.
>
> **NEW BREAKAGE POINTS**: Dragoon-containment funding squeeze; dragoon-guild jurisdictional conflict cascade; mission-creep adoption rollback.

## Politics/Institution Report

> **VERDICT**: Recommend repair. Structurally sound but under-specified at two load-bearing boundaries (dragoon-vs-mercenary and dragoon-vs-private-retinue).
>
> **CHARTER-ERA FIT**: Plausible concession with explicit tether clauses (ward-breach-declaration predicate; civic-treasury pay; ward-resident recruitment; no hire-out; magistrate chain-of-command).
>
> **FOURTH-AXIS CLASSIFICATION**: CF-0024-adjacent sub-variation on anti-monopoly-of-force axis; not fourth independent axis. [Synthesis resolution: fifth independent axis sharing substrate with CF-0024 but with distinctive shape.]
>
> **BOUNDARY SOUNDNESS**: Dragoon vs mercenary AT RISK without operational-feature boundary clauses (repair applied); dragoon vs private retinue SOUND and productively bidirectional.
>
> **RELIGIOUS RESPONSE TEMPLATE**: CF-0031 bifurcation (reform-with-protection vs abolition).

## Everyday-Life Report

> **VERDICT**: Recommend repair. Hero-drift risk MODERATE-HIGH.
>
> **PER-CLUSTER TEXTURE ASSESSMENT**: (a) thinnest where it must be thickest — lintel-marker, child-drill, retired-dragoon role, funerary treatment missing; (b) missing entirely — dowager-gossip, patron-liability ledger, tenant-cottage pattern; (c)(d)(e) silent-absence commitments entirely absent.
>
> **IN-REGISTER / OUT-OF-REGISTER LANGUAGE**: extensive word-lists specified.
>
> **ORAL-STORYTELLING SUBGENRE**: Three-layer stratification (patron-commissioned / tavern-veteran / charter-court opposition).
>
> **REPAIR RECOMMENDATIONS**: Before accept, patches required for all five clusters with silent-absence in (c)(d)(e) and concrete household-level texture in (a)(b).

## Theme/Tone Report

> **VERDICT**: Recommend repair. Tonal discipline DECORATIVE not load-bearing; dragoon material carries heaviest genre-pull of any canon yet adjudicated.
>
> **IN-REGISTER LANGUAGE**: [full word-list]
>
> **OUT-OF-REGISTER LANGUAGE**: [word-lists grouped by military-romance, heroic-sacrifice, chosen-force, paramilitary-glamour, counter-register heroic-by-inversion]
>
> **BARDIC-SUBGENRE DISCIPLINE**: Three-layer stratification — patron-commissioned (institution not individual), tavern-veteran (AES-1 scarred), anti-dragoon charter-court opposition (litigious NOT revolutionary pulp).
>
> **MISSION-CREEP FRAMING**: Bureaucratic drift NOT fascist slide. Verbs administrative; charter-court is load-bearing counterweight.
>
> **GENRE-DRIFT RISKS**: Elite-unit romance; last-stand heroics; gallant lieutenant; anti-fascist pulp inversion; paramilitary aesthetic seepage; CF-0026 collision; CF-0031 collision.

## Mystery Curator Report

> **VERDICT**: Recommend repair. Card's Phase 7b firewall check is materially insufficient.
>
> **FIREWALL ADEQUACY**: M-5 adequate; M-6 INADEQUATE (confiscation-custody proximity); M-7 INADEQUATE (retired-dragoon civic-archived reputation-accretion); M-8 INADEQUATE (perimeter-lore accretion risk); M-9 INADEQUATE-ADJACENT (civic-evidence archive custody channel); M-11 INADEQUATE (pensioner-health three-generation parallel dataset).
>
> **NEW M-ENTRY RECOMMENDATIONS**: M-13 (institutional contingency of dragoon form; passive; low safety).
>
> **LATENT BURDENS**: LB-1 through LB-4.

# Latent Burdens Logged

**Explicit latent burdens** (per Mystery Curator):
- **LB-1**: Any future CF describing dragoon confiscation-procedure must specify custody-disposition of modern crafted artifacts without triggering M-6.
- **LB-2**: Any future CF touching dragoon pensioner welfare, charter-court extradition rulings, or dragoon-family civic status must not produce three-generation longitudinal health-records in a form that parallels the scrivener's-convention dataset.
- **LB-3**: Any future CF naming a famous dragoon commander must constrain reputation to civic-tactical domain and not extend to ward-breach ontology.
- **LB-4**: Any future CF describing inter-polity extradition dispute resolution must not retroactively adjudicate whether the four adoption-stabilizers are causally necessary (M-13 preservation).

**Additional monitored exposures** (per Systems/Economy critic and cross-phase synthesis):
- **Funding-stream specificity**: deferred per adopting polity to OPEN_QUESTIONS; displacing-mechanism variants carry dragoon-containment funding squeeze breakage-point exposure.
- **Transition-cost**: 3-7 year ward-breach severity spike during buildout in small adopting polities; monitored economic exposure.
- **Reactive-adoption pressure**: black-market re-routing toward non-adopting polities may trigger defensive adoption partially defeating Stabilizer-1; monitored Charter-Era stress surface.
- **Mission-creep countergate fragility**: charter-court challenge effectiveness depends on charter-court precedent robustness and CF-0026 refuser-clergy influence per polity; M-13 tracks as bounded-unknown.
